hero.headline: LPU Online MBA in Finance: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Lovely Professional University's UGC-DEB approved online MBA with a Finance specialisation. NIRF #31 University, NAAC A+. Rs 2,00,000 standard fee, Rs 1,61,600 with 20% Student Grant. Zero-cost 24-month EMI at Rs 6,733/month. Financial Derivatives, Investment Analysis, International Finance, Risk Management, and Security Analysis in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: LPU Online MBA Finance: NIRF #31 (University), NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved, Rs 2L standard or Rs 1.61L with 20% grant, zero-cost 24-month EMI. Finance depth with Financial Derivatives, Security Analysis, Investment Analysis, International Finance, Tax Planning, and Risk Management. Strong for banking, investment, and corporate finance careers.

about.body:
The LPU Online MBA in Finance is offered by Lovely Professional University's online division, one of India's largest private universities. LPU holds NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #31 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #44 in the Management category. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The Finance specialisation delivers dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects covering Financial Derivatives, Security Analysis and Portfolio Management, Investment Analysis and Portfolio Management, International Finance, Tax Planning and Management, and Risk Management. These subjects are designed for professionals targeting roles in banking, investment advisory, corporate treasury, and financial planning.

LPU's Phagwara campus maintains industry connect with Punjab-based banking clusters and North India financial institutions. The affordable fee structure with zero-cost EMI makes LPU Finance a compelling option for working professionals seeking a credentialed finance MBA without career interruption.

sem3Subjects.name: Financial Derivatives
sem3Subjects.description: Apply futures, options, swaps, and structured derivative instruments to corporate hedging and investment strategies.

sem3Subjects.name: Security Analysis and Portfolio Management
sem3Subjects.description: Conduct equity valuation using DCF, relative valuation, and technical analysis; build and manage investment portfolios.

sem3Subjects.name: Investment Analysis and Portfolio Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply Markowitz portfolio theory, CAPM, and factor models to construct risk-adjusted investment portfolios.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Strategy and Entrepreneurship
sem3Subjects.description: Develop corporate growth strategies, evaluate M&A rationale, and design finance-led entrepreneurial ventures.

sem3Subjects.name: Generic Elective I
sem3Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the Finance specialisation.

sem3Subjects.name: Seminar on Summer Training
sem3Subjects.description: Present findings from summer training project to faculty panel, developing professional communication and analytical skills.

sem4Subjects.name: Capstone Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied finance research project on portfolio management, corporate valuation, or financial risk analysis.

sem4Subjects.name: International Finance
sem4Subjects.description: Manage foreign exchange risk, multinational capital budgeting, cross-border financing structures, and FOREX hedging.

sem4Subjects.name: Tax Planning and Management
sem4Subjects.description: Apply corporate tax planning frameworks, deferred tax accounting, and direct and indirect tax optimisation strategies.

sem4Subjects.name: Risk Management
sem4Subjects.description: Identify and quantify financial risk using VaR, stress testing, credit risk models, and enterprise risk frameworks.

sem4Subjects.name: Generic Elective II
sem4Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the Finance specialisation.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all LPU Online MBA specialisations. See the main LPU Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
Finance MBA graduates from LPU Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Commercial banking, Investment banking and capital markets, Corporate treasury, Insurance and wealth management, Financial consulting and accounting firms

**Entry-level roles:** Financial Analyst, Credit Analyst, Investment Research Analyst, Treasury Analyst, Risk Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Finance Manager, Portfolio Manager, Treasury Manager, Credit Manager, Risk Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CFO, Head of Finance, VP Treasury, Investment Director, Chief Risk Officer

**Top hiring organisations:** HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Axis Bank, SBI, Yes Bank, Bajaj Finance, HDFC Life, Max Life Insurance, Deloitte, KPMG, EY, Motilal Oswal, IIFL Wealth

skills.body:
The LPU Finance specialisation builds skills that banking and corporate finance employers test at analyst and manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Financial Derivatives: futures and options pricing, hedging strategy design, and structured product analysis for treasury and investment roles
- Security Analysis and Portfolio Management: equity valuation, DCF modelling, relative valuation, and technical analysis for investment research and advisory
- Risk Management: VaR calculation, credit risk modelling, stress testing, and enterprise risk framework design for banking and insurance roles
- International Finance: FOREX hedging, multinational capital budgeting, and cross-border financing for corporate treasury and global banking

**Applied skills:**
- Investment Analysis and Portfolio Management: Markowitz optimisation, CAPM application, and factor model-based portfolio construction for wealth management roles
- Tax Planning and Management: corporate tax structure optimisation and deferred tax accounting for corporate finance and consulting

**Soft skills:** Presenting investment recommendations to senior management, negotiating credit facilities with banks, building financial models for board-level decisions, and communicating risk exposure to audit committees.

comparisons.body:
Comparing LPU Online MBA Finance against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Finance:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Finance covers corporate finance, banking, and investment management broadly. LPU Finance is comparable in subject coverage at a lower fee of Rs 2,00,000 standard or Rs 1,61,600 with grant. LPU offers zero-cost EMI; Amity does not. For cost-sensitive students with zero-cost EMI preference, LPU Finance has a fee advantage.

**vs MUJ Online MBA Finance:** MUJ is NAAC A+ and NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000-1,80,000 with Coursera Plus. MUJ Finance carries the Manipal brand and NIRF higher ranking for business schools. LPU Finance offers Financial Derivatives and Risk Management as dedicated subjects. Both are strong options at comparable fee levels.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Financial Management:** NMIMS is NAAC A+ and carries strong Mumbai BFSI brand recognition. NMIMS Financial Management is Rs 1,60,000-1,95,000 with zero-cost EMI. For Mumbai banking and BFSI careers, NMIMS Financial Management carries stronger brand recognition. LPU Finance at Rs 1,61,600 with grant is comparable in fee and offers dedicated Financial Derivatives and Risk Management depth.

fees.body:
The LPU Online MBA in Finance offers 2 fee tracks:

**Track 1 - Standard Fee**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 2,00,000
- Semester fee: Rs 50,000 per semester across 4 semesters

**Track 2 - With 20% Student Grant**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 1,61,600 after 20% Student Grant
- Semester fee: Rs 40,400 per semester

**Zero-Cost EMI option (Track 2)**:
- 24-month zero-cost EMI: Rs 6,733 per month
- No interest charged. No processing fee from the university.
- EMI is routed through the university's marketing partner.

The 20% Student Grant is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility and applicable conditions with our counsellor before applying.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with LPU each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and grant eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Lovely Professional University (LPU) and carries the LPU name with the MBA designation. The Finance specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from LPU Online MBA Finance alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Ankit Verma
items.liked: Financial Derivatives and Risk Management subjects were directly applicable to my role at a Punjab-based cooperative bank. Zero-cost EMI made the programme genuinely affordable.
items.disliked: Faculty for some Sem 4 electives were shared with other specialisations and lacked deep finance domain focus.
items.body: I had 4 years in bank credit operations and the LPU Finance MBA helped me transition to treasury management. The Rs 1.61L fee with zero-cost EMI was the key deciding factor. LPU brand is well recognised at North India banking institutions.

items.name: Priya Kapoor
items.liked: Security Analysis and Investment Analysis subjects gave me strong portfolio management fundamentals. Tax Planning and Management was well-structured and practically applicable.
items.disliked: Peer group interaction in live sessions was limited. More case study integration from Indian banking would help.
items.body: Good programme at a very competitive fee. The zero-cost EMI was a genuine benefit. LPU brand is recognised at Delhi-NCR financial services firms. For BFSI careers in North India, LPU Finance is a strong value proposition.

items.name: Rahul Singh
items.liked: International Finance and Risk Management gave me language and frameworks for my corporate treasury role. Affordable fee with good content.
items.disliked: Placement support was limited for specialised finance roles. Self-sourcing was necessary.
items.body: Solid credential for the fee. For North India corporate finance roles, LPU is recognised. For Mumbai BFSI, NMIMS would carry stronger brand weight. For cost-conscious professionals with North India career focus, LPU Finance is the right choice.

faqs.question: How does the LPU 20% Student Grant work?
faqs.answer: The 20% Student Grant reduces the standard Rs 2,00,000 fee to Rs 1,61,600. It is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility conditions with our counsellor before applying, as grant availability may vary by intake cycle.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA zero-cost EMI genuinely interest-free?
faqs.answer: Yes. The 24-month zero-cost EMI at Rs 6,733 per month carries no interest charge and no processing fee from the university. It is routed through the university's marketing partner. Confirm current EMI terms before committing.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA the same as LPU on-campus MBA?
faqs.answer: Both are awarded by Lovely Professional University. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved and fully online. The LPU name appears on both certificates. Corporate employers treat UGC-DEB approved online MBAs as equivalent for hiring eligibility.

faqs.question: Is LPU Finance MBA suitable for banking careers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Financial Derivatives, Risk Management, and International Finance are directly applicable to banking treasury, credit, and risk management roles. The programme is well-suited for working professionals in banking looking to formalise their skills with a credentialed MBA.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA NIRF ranked?
faqs.answer: Yes. Lovely Professional University is ranked NIRF #31 (University category, 2025) and NIRF #44 in the Management category. This places LPU among the top-ranked universities offering UGC-DEB approved online MBAs in India.

chooseIf: You want a UGC-DEB approved MBA from a NIRF #31 university at Rs 1,61,600 (with grant) or Rs 2,00,000 standard.
You want 24-month zero-cost EMI at Rs 6,733 per month with no interest charges.
You are targeting banking, corporate treasury, investment analysis, or risk management careers in North India.
consider: If Mumbai BFSI brand recognition matters, NMIMS Online MBA Financial Management carries stronger recognition in Mumbai banking and financial services hiring.
If Manipal brand is preferred, MUJ Online MBA Finance with Coursera Plus is available at comparable fees.
If pan-India brand recognition is the priority, Amity Online MBA Finance at Rs 2.25L offers strong all-India hiring recognition.
